property.text: The gas cap value 16777216 MUST be identical at both enforcement points (txpool and block validation). For all implementations: txpool_cap == block_validation_cap == 16777216.

reachability.notes: If the txpool uses a different cap value than block validation, transactions in the discrepancy range could pass one check but fail the other, causing inconsistent behavior.

property.severity_justification: Inconsistent cap values between enforcement points could cause nodes to disagree on block validity, directly leading to consensus failure and network split.
property.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: Static analysis or test showing both enforcement points reference the same constant.

property.text: A transaction rejected by the txpool due to gasLimit > 16777216 MUST NOT be forwarded to block building or execution. The rejection MUST be final and the transaction MUST be dropped from the system entirely.

reachability.notes: The boundary from txpool to block builder is internal, but if a rejected transaction leaks through, it could be included in a block.

property.severity_justification: A rejected transaction leaking to block building is a defense-in-depth violation. Block validation should still catch it, but the breach indicates a serious implementation flaw.

property.text: The gas cap boundary check MUST use the correct comparison operator. The acceptance condition MUST be tx.gasLimit <= 16777216 (i.e., gasLimit == 16777216 is VALID). An off-by-one error using tx.gasLimit < 16777216 would incorrectly reject transactions with gasLimit == 16777216.

reachability.notes: Attacker can probe the boundary by submitting transactions with gasLimit = 16777216 and 16777217.

property.severity_justification: Off-by-one in the comparison operator causes either valid transactions to be rejected (denial of service) or invalid transactions to be accepted (consensus violation). Cross-client inconsistency in the comparison causes a network split.
property.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: Submit transactions with gasLimit 16777215, 16777216, and 16777217 and verify only the last is rejected.

property.text: All client implementations MUST use the same comparison semantics for the gas cap check. If client A uses (gasLimit > 16777216 => reject) and client B uses (gasLimit >= 16777216 => reject), then a transaction with gasLimit == 16777216 will be accepted by A but rejected by B, causing consensus divergence.

reachability.notes: An attacker deliberately submits a transaction with gasLimit = 16777216 to trigger the boundary condition and cause a split between inconsistent clients.

property.severity_justification: Directly causes a network split between clients with inconsistent comparison operators. Single transaction can partition the Ethereum network.
property.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: Cross-client test showing inconsistent acceptance of gasLimit = 16777216.

property.text: RLP encoding of the gasLimit field in transactions MUST be strictly validated. Non-canonical encodings (e.g., leading zeros, oversized integers) MUST be rejected uniformly across all clients. Different deserialization of the same RLP bytes MUST NOT produce different gasLimit values.

reachability.notes: An attacker crafts a block with non-canonical RLP encoding of the gasLimit field, causing different clients to decode different values.

property.severity_justification: Encoding ambiguity directly causes different clients to interpret the same block differently, leading to a network split.
property.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: Craft a block with non-canonical RLP encoding of gasLimit near the 16777216 boundary and show different parsing results across clients.

property.text: The gas cap applies regardless of the block gas limit set by validators. For all block gas limits G and for all transactions tx: the constraint tx.gasLimit <= 16777216 holds independently of G. Even if G > 16777216, each individual transaction is still capped.

reachability.notes: A malicious validator sets a very high block gas limit and includes over-cap transactions. Other validators must still reject the block.

property.severity_justification: If the per-transaction cap is confused with the block gas limit, a malicious validator could bypass EIP-7825 entirely.
property.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: Create a block with block gas limit > 16777216 containing a transaction with gasLimit > 16777216.

property.text: Block validation gas cap check MUST be efficient (O(1) per transaction, O(n) total) and MUST NOT allocate additional memory per transaction. Processing a block with the maximum number of transactions MUST NOT cause excessive resource consumption beyond the integer comparison per transaction.

reachability.notes: Attacker sends blocks with maximum transaction count to trigger worst-case validation cost.

property.severity_justification: An inefficient validation loop could slow down block processing, causing validators to miss attestation deadlines. Limited by existing block gas limit constraints.
property.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: Benchmark block validation time with maximum transaction count blocks.

property.text: Block builder MUST NOT be able to circumvent the gas cap by constructing blocks directly without going through the txpool validation path. Block-level validation (SG-002) MUST independently re-check every transaction's gasLimit regardless of source.

reachability.notes: A malicious block builder (compromised validator or MEV builder) directly constructs blocks bypassing the txpool. Block validation must catch this.

property.severity_justification: If block validation can be skipped by block builders, the entire gas cap enforcement is compromised for proposers.
property.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: Construct a block via Engine API with an over-cap transaction and show block validation catches it.

property.text: Transaction replacement (e.g., EIP-1559 fee bumping) MUST re-validate the gasLimit of the replacement transaction against the cap. A valid original transaction replaced with one having gasLimit > 16777216 MUST be rejected.

reachability.notes: Attacker submits a valid transaction then replaces it with one having gasLimit > 16777216, attempting a TOCTOU bypass.

property.severity_justification: TOCTOU in transaction replacement could allow over-cap transactions to enter the mempool, though block validation provides a second check.
property.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: Submit tx with gasLimit = 16777216, then replace with gasLimit = 16777217 and show the replacement is rejected.

property.text: The gasLimit field is covered by the transaction signature. For all transactions tx: any modification of tx.gasLimit after signing MUST invalidate the signature. External tampering of the gasLimit in transit MUST be detectable.

reachability.notes: A P2P peer could attempt to modify the gasLimit field of a transaction in transit. Signature verification must detect this.

property.severity_justification: If gasLimit is not covered by the signature, a man-in-the-middle P2P peer could increase it beyond the cap.
property.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: Modify a transaction's gasLimit in transit and show signature verification fails.

property.text: P2P peers that repeatedly send transactions with gasLimit > 16777216 MUST be penalized through peer scoring mechanisms and eventually disconnected. The penalty MUST be applied after a configurable threshold of violations.

reachability.notes: An attacker floods peers with invalid transactions. Without peer penalization, the bandwidth cost is borne entirely by the victim.

property.severity_justification: The gas cap check itself is cheap (integer comparison), but high-volume flooding without peer disconnection could degrade network performance.

property.text: For all inputs to the ModExp precompile: multiplication_complexity >= 16. This holds for both the small path (max_length <= 32) where complexity is exactly 16, and the large path (max_length > 32) where complexity = 2 * ceil(max_length/8)^2 >= 2 * 5^2 = 50.

reachability.notes: Attacker controls base_length and modulus_length via smart contract CALL inputs. If complexity can be driven below 16, gas underpricing occurs.

property.severity_justification: If multiplication_complexity can be reduced below 16, the gas cost formula undercharges, enabling DoS via cheap-but-expensive ModExp calls across all nodes processing the block.
property.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: Find inputs where multiplication_complexity < 16 and demonstrate gas underpricing.

property.text: For all inputs where max(base_length, modulus_length) <= 32: multiplication_complexity MUST equal exactly 16. No other value is acceptable for the small operand path.

reachability.notes: Attacker deploys a contract that calls ModExp with max_length = 32 exactly at the branch boundary.

property.severity_justification: Incorrect branching at max_length = 32 boundary causes consensus divergence between clients using different formula paths.
property.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: Call ModExp with max_length = 32 and max_length = 33, compare gas costs across clients.

property.text: For all inputs where max(base_length, modulus_length) > 32: multiplication_complexity MUST equal 2 * ceil(max_length/8)^2. The ceil operation and squaring MUST use overflow-checked or arbitrary-precision arithmetic.

reachability.notes: Attacker provides extremely large base_length or modulus_length to trigger integer overflow in words^2 computation.

property.severity_justification: Integer overflow in multiplication_complexity wraps the gas cost to a small value, making an extremely expensive operation nearly free. This enables network-wide DoS.
property.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: Provide base_length such that ceil(base_length/8)^2 overflows the implementation's integer type.

property.text: For all inputs where exponent_length <= 32 and exponent == 0: iteration_count MUST equal exactly 1. This is the base case that prevents division by zero or zero-cost execution.

reachability.notes: Attacker calls ModExp with exponent = 0 to test if iteration_count is correctly set to 1.

property.severity_justification: If iteration_count is 0 for exponent = 0, the gas cost formula produces 0 (before the floor of 500), which with the max(500, ...) is caught. But if the implementation uses iteration_count in other ways (e.g., as a loop bound), it could cause issues. Cross-client consistency at this edge case is critical for consensus.
property.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: Call ModExp with exponent = 0 and compare gas cost across all client implementations.

property.text: For all inputs where exponent_length <= 32 and exponent != 0: iteration_count MUST equal bit_length(exponent) - 1. The bit_length computation MUST be consistent across all clients.

reachability.notes: Attacker calls ModExp with various exponent values to probe bit_length calculation consistency.

property.severity_justification: Inconsistent bit_length computation across clients causes different gas costs for the same inputs, leading to consensus failure.
property.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: Call ModExp with exponent values at power-of-2 boundaries and compare gas costs across clients.

property.text: For all inputs where exponent_length > 32: iteration_count MUST equal 16 * (exponent_length - 32) + bit_length(top_32_bytes_of_exponent), and the result MUST be at least 1. The large exponent multiplier MUST be 16 (not 8 as in EIP-2565).

reachability.notes: Attacker provides exponent_length > 32 to trigger the large exponent path and test the multiplier value.

property.severity_justification: If the multiplier is 8 (EIP-2565) instead of 16 (EIP-7883), gas cost is halved for large exponents, enabling network-wide DoS via underpriced ModExp calls. Cross-client inconsistency causes consensus divergence.
property.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: Call ModExp with exponent_length = 64 and show that the gas cost uses multiplier 16, not 8.

property.text: For all inputs where exponent_length > 32: the 16 * (exponent_length - 32) computation MUST use overflow-checked arithmetic. For very large exponent_length values, integer overflow could wrap the iteration_count to a small value.

reachability.notes: Attacker provides a very large exponent_length to trigger overflow in 16 * (exponent_length - 32).

property.severity_justification: Integer overflow in iteration_count calculation reduces gas cost to near-zero for an expensive operation, enabling network-wide DoS through resource exhaustion.
property.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: Provide exponent_length near uint64 max / 16 to trigger overflow.

property.text: For all ModExp inputs: the final gas cost MUST equal max(500, floor(multiplication_complexity * iteration_count)). The division by 3 present in EIP-2565 MUST NOT be applied in EIP-7883.

reachability.notes: If a client still applies the EIP-2565 division by 3, gas cost is 3x lower than intended, enabling DoS.

property.severity_justification: Applying the old EIP-2565 formula (with /3) triples the attacker's computational leverage, enabling network-wide resource exhaustion. Cross-client inconsistency causes consensus divergence.
property.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: Call ModExp and compare gas cost with and without division by 3. Show divergence across clients.

property.text: For all ModExp inputs: the gas cost MUST be >= 500. The minimum gas floor of 500 (raised from 200) prevents abuse through many small ModExp calls.

reachability.notes: Attacker deploys a contract that makes many sequential ModExp calls with minimal inputs to maximize call count within gas limit.

property.severity_justification: With gas cap of 16777216 (EIP-7825) and minimum ModExp cost of 500, the maximum number of calls per transaction is ~33,554. If the minimum were lower, attack surface increases proportionally.
property.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: Deploy a contract making repeated ModExp calls at minimum gas and measure actual CPU time vs gas consumed.

property.text: The ModExp gas cost formula MUST produce identical results across all client implementations for all possible inputs. For any given (base_length, modulus_length, exponent_length, exponent), the gas cost MUST be deterministic and identical.

reachability.notes: Any difference in gas calculation between clients causes one client to run out of gas while another succeeds, changing the transaction receipt and breaking consensus.

property.severity_justification: Gas cost inconsistency across clients causes different state roots after executing the same block, directly breaking consensus.
property.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: Run identical ModExp calls across all Ethereum clients and compare gas consumption.

property.text: Integer arithmetic in the multiplication_complexity computation (ceil, words^2, 2*words^2) and iteration_count computation (16*(exponent_length-32), bit_length) MUST NOT overflow. Implementations MUST use sufficient precision (at least 256-bit or arbitrary precision) for these calculations.

reachability.notes: Attacker provides extremely large base_length, modulus_length, or exponent_length values designed to overflow intermediate computations in the gas cost formula.

property.severity_justification: Integer overflow in gas cost calculation wraps the cost to a small or zero value, allowing an extremely expensive computation for minimal gas. This enables network-wide DoS affecting all nodes.
property.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: Demonstrate integer overflow in gas cost computation with crafted inputs.

property.text: The multiplication_complexity * iteration_count product MUST NOT overflow. If both factors are large, their product could overflow even if each factor individually fits in a machine integer.

reachability.notes: Attacker crafts inputs where both multiplication_complexity and iteration_count are large, causing their product to overflow.

property.severity_justification: Product overflow wraps gas cost to a small value, enabling free execution of extremely expensive operations across all network nodes.
property.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: Find (base_length, exponent_length) pair where complexity * iterations overflows.

property.text: The ModExp precompile MUST validate input sizes before allocating memory buffers. For very large base_length or modulus_length values, memory allocation MUST be bounded. EIP-7823 upper bounds MUST be enforced before memory allocation occurs.

reachability.notes: Attacker provides extremely large base_length or modulus_length causing the node to attempt allocating gigabytes of memory before the gas check.

property.severity_justification: Memory exhaustion can crash validator nodes, causing them to go offline and potentially affecting network finality.
property.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: Call ModExp with base_length = 2^32 and measure memory allocation before gas deduction.

property.text: The branch condition for multiplication_complexity (max_length <= 32 vs max_length > 32) MUST be implemented identically across all clients. At max_length = 32: complexity = 16 (small path). At max_length = 33: words = ceil(33/8) = 5, complexity = 2 * 25 = 50 (large path). The transition is discontinuous but well-defined.

reachability.notes: Attacker specifically targets the max_length = 32/33 boundary to expose differences in client implementations.

property.severity_justification: Inconsistency at the branch boundary between clients causes different gas costs for the same inputs, leading to consensus failure.
property.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: Cross-client test calling ModExp with max_length = 32 and max_length = 33 and comparing gas consumed.

property.text: The EIP-7883 gas cost formula MUST NOT contain a division by 3. The formula is: gas = max(500, floor(multiplication_complexity * iteration_count)). Any implementation still using the EIP-2565 formula floor(multiplication_complexity * iteration_count / 3) is incorrect.

reachability.notes: This is the most critical change in EIP-7883. If any client retains the /3 divisor, gas costs are 3x lower than intended.

property.severity_justification: Retaining the /3 divisor reduces gas costs by 3x, enabling DoS attacks. Cross-client inconsistency directly causes consensus divergence.
property.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: Call ModExp and show that gas consumed matches max(500, floor(complexity * iterations)) without /3.

property.text: For all ModExp inputs where the computed gas (floor(multiplication_complexity * iteration_count)) < 500: the charged gas MUST be exactly 500. The minimum floor of 500 MUST NOT be circumvented by any input combination.

reachability.notes: Attacker tries various small input combinations to find a case where gas < 500 is charged.

property.severity_justification: Bypassing the minimum floor allows cheaper ModExp calls than intended, increasing the number of calls possible within a single transaction and amplifying DoS potential.
property.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: Find inputs where gas charged is less than 500.

property.text: ModExp precompile execution time MUST be bounded and proportional to the gas charged. For all inputs: actual_execution_time <= k * gas_charged for some constant k. No input combination should cause disproportionate CPU usage relative to gas cost.

reachability.notes: Attacker searches for input combinations where actual execution time is disproportionately high relative to gas cost.

property.severity_justification: Disproportionate execution time vs gas cost enables DoS across all nodes processing the block containing the transaction.
property.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: Benchmark ModExp with various inputs and show ratio of execution time to gas cost. Identify outlier inputs.

property.text: With EIP-7825 gas cap of 16777216 and EIP-7883 minimum ModExp gas of 500, the maximum number of ModExp calls per transaction is bounded by 16777216 / 500 = 33,554 (approximately). The total CPU time for this many minimum-cost ModExp calls MUST remain within acceptable block processing time limits.

reachability.notes: Attacker deploys a contract that calls ModExp 33,554 times with minimum-gas inputs within a single transaction at the gas cap.

property.severity_justification: If each minimum-cost ModExp call takes significant CPU time, 33,554 calls could slow block processing beyond acceptable limits, causing attestation delays.
property.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: Deploy contract making maximum ModExp calls at minimum gas and measure total block processing time.

property.text: The ModExp precompile MUST check that sufficient gas is available BEFORE executing the modular exponentiation. If available gas < computed gas cost, the precompile MUST revert without performing the computation.

reachability.notes: If gas check happens after execution, the node performs expensive computation without being compensated.

property.severity_justification: Executing ModExp before deducting gas allows an attacker to consume CPU without paying for it, enabling DoS.
property.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: Call ModExp with insufficient gas and demonstrate that execution is not performed.

property.text: The iteration_count MUST be >= 1 for all inputs (non-trivial). The max(iteration_count, 1) clamp MUST be applied for the large exponent path. This prevents zero-cost execution.

reachability.notes: Attacker provides inputs designed to make iteration_count = 0 before the max(1, ...) clamp.

property.severity_justification: If iteration_count is 0, complexity * 0 = 0, and gas = max(500, 0) = 500 which is correct. However, if the clamp is missing and the value is used elsewhere, it could cause issues.
property.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: Provide inputs where bit_length(top_32_bytes) = 0 and exponent_length = 33 to get 16 * 1 + 0 = 16 >= 1.

property.text: The interaction between EIP-7825 gas cap and the EVM 63/64 rule for CALL gas forwarding MUST NOT produce unexpected behavior. For a transaction with gasLimit = 16777216 making a CALL, the forwarded gas MUST be floor(63/64 * available_gas) as per standard EVM rules. The cap does not change gas forwarding semantics.

reachability.notes: Interaction between the gas cap and gas forwarding rules could produce edge cases not present at lower gas limits.

property.severity_justification: The gas cap (2^24) is well within uint64 range and the 63/64 rule has no special behavior at this value. Risk is theoretical.

property.text: Existing mempool flooding protections (size limits, fee-based eviction) MUST remain effective under EIP-7825. Transactions with gasLimit = 16777216 MUST NOT receive preferential treatment in mempool eviction compared to transactions with lower gasLimit values.

reachability.notes: Attacker floods the mempool with max-gasLimit transactions to displace legitimate lower-gas transactions.

property.severity_justification: Standard mempool flooding is not novel to EIP-7825. Existing protections apply. The gas cap constrains maximum transaction size but does not change eviction policies.

property.text: The ModExp precompile MUST correctly handle edge case inputs: base_length = 0, modulus_length = 0, exponent_length = 0, all-zero inputs, single-byte inputs, max_length at the 32/33 boundary, and very large length values. Each edge case MUST produce a deterministic gas cost and result consistent across all clients.

reachability.notes: Attacker systematically tests edge cases looking for divergence between client implementations.

property.severity_justification: Any edge case producing different gas costs across clients causes consensus failure when a block containing such a transaction is processed.
property.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: Cross-client test with edge case inputs showing gas cost divergence.

property.text: The EIP-7883 gas formula MUST produce a gas cost >= the minimum gas floor for inputs where the formula result (complexity * iterations) < 500. Specifically: for max_length <= 32 and exponent == 0: gas = max(500, floor(16 * 1)) = max(500, 16) = 500.

reachability.notes: Verifies that the minimum floor is correctly applied for the cheapest possible ModExp call.

property.severity_justification: Failure to apply the minimum floor at the cheapest input combination increases DoS potential by reducing per-call cost.
property.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: Call ModExp with smallest inputs and verify 500 gas is charged.

property.text: P2P messages containing transactions or blocks MUST be independently validated regardless of the peer's history or reputation. A previously trusted peer that sends a transaction with gasLimit > 16777216 MUST have that transaction rejected. No cached validation results from peers may be trusted.

reachability.notes: An attacker compromises a previously well-behaved peer and begins sending invalid transactions.

property.severity_justification: Trusting cached validation results from peers could allow invalid transactions to bypass local validation.
property.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: Show that switching a previously trusted peer to send invalid transactions does not bypass validation.

property.text: The ModExp precompile MUST compute gas cost BEFORE allocating memory for operands. The order of operations MUST be: (1) parse lengths from input, (2) compute gas cost, (3) check gas availability, (4) allocate memory and read operands, (5) execute. Steps 4 and 5 MUST NOT occur if step 3 fails.

reachability.notes: If memory is allocated before gas check, an attacker can trigger OOM by providing very large lengths with insufficient gas.

property.severity_justification: Allocating memory before gas check enables memory exhaustion attacks that can crash validator nodes.
property.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: Call ModExp with large lengths and minimal gas; show that no memory allocation occurs before gas rejection.

property.text: For EIP-7883 formula correctness at specific test vectors: (1) base_length=1, mod_length=1, exp_length=1, exp=2: complexity=16, iterations=1, gas=max(500,16)=500. (2) base_length=64, mod_length=64, exp_length=64, exp with bit_length=256: complexity=2*8^2=128, iterations=16*(64-32)+255=767, gas=max(500,floor(128*767))=max(500,98176)=98176.

reachability.notes: Concrete test vectors that all client implementations must agree on.

property.severity_justification: Failure to match expected gas costs at specific test vectors indicates formula implementation errors that will cause consensus divergence.
property.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: Cross-client execution of these exact test vectors and comparison of gas results.

property.text: The EIP-7825 gas cap enforcement MUST be activated only after the Fusaka hard fork block. Before the fork, no per-transaction gas cap is enforced by EIP-7825. After the fork, all transactions MUST comply. The activation boundary MUST be consistent across all clients.

reachability.notes: If one client activates EIP-7825 at block N and another at block N+1, transactions with gasLimit > 16777216 at block N cause a split.

property.severity_justification: Fork activation inconsistency directly causes a network split at the fork boundary.
property.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: Submit a gasLimit > 16777216 transaction at the exact fork block and show cross-client behavior.

property.text: The EIP-7883 ModExp gas cost formula MUST be activated only after the Fusaka hard fork block. Before the fork, the EIP-2565 formula (with /3 and minimum 200) applies. After the fork, the EIP-7883 formula (without /3 and minimum 500) applies. The activation boundary MUST be consistent across all clients.

reachability.notes: If one client uses the new formula before the fork block or the old formula after, gas costs diverge, causing consensus failure.

property.severity_justification: Gas cost formula mismatch at the fork boundary causes different state roots, directly breaking consensus.
property.bug_bounty_notes: PoC: Execute ModExp at the exact fork block and compare gas costs between pre/post-fork formula.

property.text: For all inputs to cell_to_coset_evals (SG-001), every byte segment cell[i*BYTES_PER_FIELD_ELEMENT : (i+1)*BYTES_PER_FIELD_ELEMENT] MUST represent a value strictly less than BLS_MODULUS. If bytes_to_bls_field receives a byte segment >= BLS_MODULUS, it MUST reject with an error, never silently reduce modulo BLS_MODULUS.

reachability.notes: An attacker can submit a blob via P2P containing byte sequences that encode values >= BLS_MODULUS. If bytes_to_bls_field silently wraps around, different client implementations may produce different field elements from the same blob, causing consensus divergence.

property.severity_justification: Silent modular reduction vs. rejection across clients would produce different polynomial commitments and proofs from identical blobs, causing a consensus split across the entire network.
property.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC blob with a field element encoding >= BLS_MODULUS that produces different behavior in two or more client implementations.

property.text: cell_to_coset_evals (SG-001) and coset_evals_to_cell (SG-002) MUST be exact inverses: for all valid CosetEvals c, cell_to_coset_evals(coset_evals_to_cell(c)) == c, and for all valid Cells c', coset_evals_to_cell(cell_to_coset_evals(c')) == c'.

reachability.notes: Round-trip encoding/decoding failures would corrupt cell data along the entire blob processing pipeline. Attacker-submitted blobs traverse this path.

property.severity_justification: Non-invertible serialization corrupts all downstream polynomial evaluations, KZG proofs, and data availability guarantees. Different round-trip behavior across clients causes consensus divergence.
property.bug_bounty_notes: Requires a field element value that round-trips to a different value in at least one client.

property.text: The output of cell_to_coset_evals (SG-001) MUST contain exactly FIELD_ELEMENTS_PER_CELL elements. No more, no fewer, regardless of cell content.

reachability.notes: Cells received over P2P from peers could have unexpected sizes if validation is missing. Incorrect element count would cause FFT dimension mismatches.

property.severity_justification: Incorrect element count would cause crashes or undefined behavior in FFT operations that require power-of-2 sized inputs.
property.bug_bounty_notes: Requires crafted cell that produces wrong element count in a specific client.

property.text: _fft_field (SG-003) MUST only accept inputs where len(vals) is a power of 2. If len(vals) is not a power of 2, the function MUST reject the input before performing any computation.

reachability.notes: If an attacker can influence the size of data reaching FFT (e.g., through malformed cells that bypass size validation), non-power-of-2 inputs would cause infinite recursion or incorrect splits.

property.severity_justification: Non-power-of-2 input to recursive FFT causes infinite recursion (stack overflow crash) or incorrect recursive splits producing wrong results.
property.bug_bounty_notes: Requires PoC showing a code path where external input reaches FFT with non-power-of-2 length.

property.text: When inv=True, fft_field (SG-004) MUST apply the normalization factor 1/N (where N = len(vals)) to each output element. The inverse FFT followed by forward FFT MUST be the identity transformation: fft(ifft(vals)) == vals.

reachability.notes: Inverse FFT is used in polynomial_eval_to_coeff (SG-007) which processes attacker-submitted blob data. Missing normalization would produce incorrect polynomial coefficients.

property.severity_justification: Missing 1/N normalization in inverse FFT would produce incorrect polynomial coefficients for every blob, causing all KZG proofs to be invalid and breaking data availability across the network.
property.bug_bounty_notes: Requires demonstrating a client that omits or incorrectly computes the normalization factor.

property.text: coset_fft_field (SG-005) with shift_factor = PRIMITIVE_ROOT_OF_UNITY MUST evaluate polynomials on a coset that does not intersect the FFT evaluation domain. Specifically, for all x in the coset domain and all y in the FFT domain, x != y. This guarantees that division by the vanishing polynomial in recovery (SG-023) never encounters division by zero.

reachability.notes: An attacker providing partial cells for recovery triggers coset FFT evaluation. If the coset intersects the FFT domain, division by zero occurs during recovery, crashing the node.

property.severity_justification: Division by zero during polynomial recovery would crash the node, preventing it from recovering data for data availability. If PRIMITIVE_ROOT_OF_UNITY is incorrectly chosen, all recovery operations network-wide would fail.
property.bug_bounty_notes: Requires demonstrating that PRIMITIVE_ROOT_OF_UNITY choice in a specific client leads to coset-FFT domain intersection.

property.text: The Fiat-Shamir challenge r computed by compute_verify_cell_kzg_proof_batch_challenge (SG-006) MUST include ALL verifier-relevant data in the hash input: domain separator, FIELD_ELEMENTS_PER_BLOB, FIELD_ELEMENTS_PER_CELL, len(commitments), len(cell_indices), all commitments, all commitment_indices, all cell_indices, all coset evaluations, and all proofs.

reachability.notes: An attacker submitting cells and proofs for batch verification controls all hash inputs. If any verifier-relevant field is omitted from the hash, the attacker can substitute invalid data that passes verification.

property.severity_justification: Omitting any field from the Fiat-Shamir hash input allows an attacker to craft inputs where invalid proofs pass batch verification, completely breaking data availability guarantees.
property.bug_bounty_notes: Requires identifying a client implementation that omits a field from the Fiat-Shamir hash computation.

property.text: The domain separator RANDOM_CHALLENGE_KZG_CELL_BATCH_DOMAIN used in compute_verify_cell_kzg_proof_batch_challenge (SG-006) MUST be globally unique across all Ethereum protocols using the same hash function. No other protocol context may use the same domain separator value.

reachability.notes: Cross-protocol replay attacks become possible if the domain separator collides with another protocol. An attacker could replay a valid Fiat-Shamir challenge from one context in another.

property.severity_justification: Domain separator collision enables cross-protocol Fiat-Shamir replay attacks, potentially allowing invalid proofs to pass verification by reusing challenges from a different protocol context.
property.bug_bounty_notes: Requires identifying a domain separator collision with another deployed protocol.

property.text: The hash input order in compute_verify_cell_kzg_proof_batch_challenge (SG-006) MUST be deterministic and canonical across all client implementations. All clients MUST serialize fields in the identical order: domain, FIELD_ELEMENTS_PER_BLOB, FIELD_ELEMENTS_PER_CELL, len(commitments), len(cell_indices), commitments, then for each k: commitment_indices[k], cell_indices[k], coset_evals[k], proofs[k].

reachability.notes: Different serialization order would produce different challenge r values across clients, causing batch verification to produce different results.

property.severity_justification: Non-deterministic hash input ordering across clients produces different Fiat-Shamir challenges, causing consensus divergence on batch verification results for identical inputs.
property.bug_bounty_notes: Requires demonstrating two clients that serialize the Fiat-Shamir hash input in different orders.

property.text: polynomial_eval_to_coeff (SG-007) MUST accept exactly FIELD_ELEMENTS_PER_BLOB evaluations as input. Inputs of any other length MUST be rejected.

reachability.notes: The number of evaluations is derived from blob size. If blob_to_polynomial produces a non-standard number of evaluations, this invariant would be violated.

property.severity_justification: Incorrect evaluation count would produce polynomials of wrong degree, causing all subsequent KZG proof operations to be algebraically invalid.
property.bug_bounty_notes: Requires a code path where blob_to_polynomial returns non-FIELD_ELEMENTS_PER_BLOB evaluations.
